hypothesis: A single landmarked checkout with a persistent order summary minimizes context switching while headings, fieldsets, error summary, and skip or landmark navigation preserve nonvisual orientation.
interaction_paradigm: A single-page form with all contact, delivery address, shipping, synthetic payment, review, placement, and confirmation sections present and landmarked, plus a sticky order summary at mobile widths.

human_use.rationale: The Artifact is a human-operated mobile checkout. Its success depends on discoverability, navigation, input burden, error recovery, feedback, accessibility, touch ergonomics, interruption recovery, perceived latency, destructive-action safety, and cognitive load across cross-disability use.

qualitative_evaluation.note: Qualitative use-friction was not converted into an objective ergonomics claim.

commands.build: Authored standalone local index.html, styles.css, and app.js within loop-01; no external resource, server, dependency, or network was used.
commands.judge: Not run; independent qualitative judging is intentionally pending.

lesson.trigger: The first objective attempts exposed harness-visible incompatibilities in a text-state control, shipping hook text, and disabled-button focus-outline measurement.
lesson.action: Use a fillable text control for state, make each shipping hook itself expose its full option text while synchronizing checked state, and retain a pinned high-contrast outline on the final action.
lesson.evidence: Attempt 01 failed five gates, attempt 02 failed two gates, and the unchanged frozen harness passed all seven gates on attempt 03.

stop_reason: Exactly one candidate Loop was requested. All frozen objective gates pass; no independent qualitative judge was run, so promotion is not claimed.
